data.title: Enlight Renewable Energy reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Enlight Renewable Energy press release (NASDAQ:ENLT): Q4 Net income of $8m, down 48% year over year
  • Revenue of $104M (+35.1% Y/Y) beats by $22.46M.

  • Financial guidance

    • Total revenues and income are expected to range between $490m and $510m, a 25% increase (from the midpoint) from 2024 results. Of the projected revenues and income, 38% are expected to be denominated in ILS, 35% in EUR, and 27% in USD.
    • Adjusted EBITDA is expected to range between $360m and $380m, a 28% increase (from the midpoint) from 2024 results.
    • Approximately 90% of the electricity volumes expected to be generated in 2025 will be sold at fixed prices through PPAs or hedges.

data.title: Commodity Roundup: Oil extends gains, gold futures rise to near record highs on trade war fears
data.description:
Oil prices rose on Wednesday, extending gains to a third-consecutive session amid growing supply worries, while gold prices gained on persistent uncertainty surrounding U.S. trade tariffs.

"If recent U.S. sanctions against a large share of the Russian shadow tanker fleet are effective, it would likely force Russian crude to use Western shipping services. That would ensure Russian crude trades at, or below, the current cap," ING analysts noted.

"This, however, would likely be short-lived as Russia and buyers will find ways around sanctions," they added.

"Russia, for example, is likely to increase the size of its non-sanction shadow fleet. To be sure, it might seem counterintuitive to see the price cap tighten at a time when the U.S. has started talks with Russia on a peace deal to end the war in Ukraine."

Brent crude (CO1:COM) was up +0.47% to $76.17 a barrel, and WTI (CL1:COM) gained +0.63% to $72.28 by 6:00 am ET.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

President Donald Trump's plans to impose tariffs of around 25% on auto imports as well as semiconductors and pharmaceuticals shipped to the United States as early as April 2, has meanwhile fuelled safe-haven demand in gold.

The yellow metal (XAUUSD:CUR) has gained about 12% so far this year, with analysts expecting prices to go even higher benefiting from a "full-blown trade war scenario".

Spot gold (XAUUSD:CUR) was trading +0.35% higher at $2,944.20/oz.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

Elsewhere, base metal prices were mixed, with LME three-month copper flat at $9,475 a metric ton and LME three-month aluminum up 1% at $2,968.50 a ton.

The market is waiting for catalysts to significantly push industrial metal prices in either direction, WSJ reported, citing a note from RBC Capital Markets.

The upcoming Chinese Two Sessions meeting—the annual plenary sessions of two major political bodies—in early March could unveil economic growth plans and potential stimulus measures, further boosting demand, RBC added.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow futures inch lower, day after S&P 500 touches record high
data.description:
Stock index futures inched lower on Wednesday, a day after the S&P 500 notched up a record high, while investors assessed more tariff plans of U.S. President Donald Trump.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) were unchanged, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.1%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.57%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 4.31%.

The S&P 500 finished at a closing high Tuesday after investors spent a choppy session with their attention on geopolitics and potential deal-making.

"Talks over Ukraine were an important catalyst for yesterday’s advance, as hopes for an end to the conflict helped power the European advance, where equities have seen a clear outperformance against their global counterparts so far this year," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

Trump's trade policies continued to dominate the headlines, as he said he would probably announce 25% tariffs on imports of cars, semiconductors, and pharmaceuticals on April 2.

"Trump rapidly retreated from trade taxes that would be very visible to US consumers—Gen Z would notice tariff-induced inflation for avocado on toast. Less visible taxes (like aluminum) have endured," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

The new proposals are partially visible—taxing new cars raises second-hand car prices and the cost of car insurance—but probably sufficiently disguised as to prevent a repeat retreat, Donovan added.

On the economic side, the January housing starts and permits and the Atlanta Fed's business inflation expectations are due on Wednesday.

The housing starts are expected to come in at 1.390M, while the building permits at 1.460M.

The FOMC meeting minutes are due later in the day.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Wednesday: BMBL, GB, and more
data.description:
Stock futures inched higher early Wednesday after the S&P 500 closed at a record high, with investors navigating geopolitical and trade uncertainties while awaiting the Federal Reserve's meeting minutes for further policy insights.

Here are some of Wednesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Global Blue (NYSE:GB) shares soared 17% after Shift4 Payments (NYSE:FOUR) announced a $2.5B acquisition deal, offering $7.50 per share in cash, a 15% premium to GB’s last close. The deal, expected to close by Q3, will integrate GB’s tax refund and currency conversion solutions into Shift4’s payments platform. Despite the expansion move, Shift4 shares dropped 9% post-announcement.

Biggest stock losers

  • Bumble (NASDAQ:BMBL) shares plunged 18% after Q4 results revealed a decline in Total Average Revenue per Paying User (ARPPU) to $20.58 from $22.64 a year ago, with Q1 2025 revenue projected to decline 7%-10% Y/Y to $242M-$248M, falling short of the $255.48M consensus; Bumble App revenue is expected to decrease 6%-8% to $198M-$202M, while adjusted EBITDA is forecasted at $60M-$63M (25% margin), as the company refocuses on product momentum, revenue strategy, and safety enhancements, discontinuing Fruitz and Official, and anticipates a short-term loss of 100K-120K paying users.
  • Arista Networks (NYSE:ANET) shares dropped 4% despite the company delivering better-than-expected Q4 results and guidance, driven by strong AI-related spending. For 1Q25, the company projects revenue between $1.93B and $1.97B, with the $1.95B midpoint exceeding the $1.91B consensus. Adjusted gross margins are expected to be around 63%.

data.title: MTU Aero Engines AG reports FY results
data.description:
• MTU Aero Engines AG press release (OTCPK:MTUAF): FY adjusted revenue of €7.5B (+19.0% Y/Y).
  • Adjusted EBIT1 for 2024 was up 28% on the previous year’s figure of €818 million, exceeding the one-billion-euro mark for the first time at €1,050 million.
  • The adjusted EBIT margin was 14.0%, compared with 12.9% in 2023.
  • Adjusted net income rose 29%, reaching a new record high at €764 million

data.title: ALK-Abelló A/S reports FY results
data.description:
• ALK-Abelló A/S press release (OTCPK:AKBLF): FY EBIT was DKK 1,091M
  • Revenue of DKK 5.5B (+15% Y/Y)
  • Tablet sales increased by 24% to DKK 2,851 million (2,296) on growth in all sales regions. European tablet sales sustained momentum with 31% growth, driven by higher volumes and improved pricing.
  • Sales of SCIT and SLIT-drops increased by 6% to DKK 2,052 (1,939) on solid performance in Europe. Sales of Other products increased by 7% to DKK 634 million (589), driven by Jext®.

  • 2025 financial outlook

    • Revenue is expected to grow by 9 -13% primarily on volume-driven growth across sales regions and product groups. Tablet remain key to growth.
    • The EBIT margin is expected to improve by 5 percentage points to around 25%, driven by revenue growth, improving gross margin, and optimisations.

data.title: Castellum AB (publ) reports FY results
data.description:
• Castellum AB (publ) press release (OTCPK:CWQXF): FY Net operating income totalled SEK 6,786 M 
  • Income totalled SEK 9,849 M (9,806), of which SEK 65 M (0) pertained to estimated indemnity.
  • Net investments totalled SEK -634 M (-1,460), of which SEK 2,502 M (3,410) pertained to new construction, extensions and reconstructions, SEK 67 M (336) to acquisitions and SEK -3,203 M (-5,206) to sales.

data.title: Sanofi to sell 50% stake in consumer health unit to CD&R
data.description:
Sanofi (NASDAQ:SNY) has entered a share purchase agreement in relation to the sale of a 50% controlling stake in Opella, its consumer healthcare business, to private equity firm CD&R.

The terms of the transaction remain unchanged from those previously disclosed, and closing is expected to take place in the second of 2025 at the earliest.

Bpifrance is expected to participate as a minority shareholder with a c.2% stake in Opella, with Sanofi remaining a significant shareholder.

Sanofi first entered into negotiations with CD&R for the potential sale in October 2024. Bloomberg, at the time, had reported the deal value to be around EUR 15B, or $16.4B.

Headquartered in France, Opella sells over-the-counter and vitamins, minerals, and supplements, with a portfolio of 100 brands including Allegra, Doliprane, Novanight, Icy Hot, and Dulcolax. It manages 13 manufacturing sites and four research and innovation centers.

data.title: Google to pay €326M to settle tax case in Italy - report
data.description:
Milan's prosecutors said they had requested to drop a case against the European unit of Alphabet's (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) Google after the company agreed to pay €326M (about $340M) to settle a tax claim, Reuters reported.

The settlement spans the period between 2015 and 2019 and covers sanctions, penalties and interest, the report added, citing a statement from prosecutors.

Google did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

In June 2024, Italy requested Google to pay €1B (around $1.07B) in unpaid taxes and penalties, the report noted.

The Milan prosecutors alleged that Google did not submit and pay taxes on revenue generated in Italy, based on claims of the digital infrastructure Google has in the European country, according to the report.

In 2017, Google paid €306M euros to settle a previous case which found that the company had a permanent presence in Italy, the report added.
